ISRAELI PM NETANYAHU STATED THAT IRAN IS NO LONGER THE SAME IRAN, ADDING THAT ISRAEL AIMS TO PREVENT IRAN FROM MOVING ITS NUCLEAR AND BALLISTIC PROJECTS UNDERGROUND.

data.title: Restolabs Brings Enterprise-Level Conversion Tactics to Independent Restaurants
data.description: Restolabs, based in New York, announced on March 12, 2026, that it is expanding its services to offer restaurants a comprehensive digital storefront solution. This includes SEO-optimized websites and custom branded mobile apps aimed at helping restaurants leverage customer ownership as a key growth strategy. The move marks a shift from solely offering online ordering services to providing a complete digital package for restaurant businesses.

data.title: Polychain invests in VeryAI's $10M funding to develop palm-scan ID on Solana for crypto platforms, prioritizing privacy.
data.description: A new startup has developed a biometric system for smartphones with the goal of assisting crypto platforms in identifying artificial intelligence-generated accounts. This system maintains user privacy by utilizing onchain verification methods. The technology is designed to combat fraudulent activities within the crypto space and enhance security measures.

data.title: Bitcoin's funding rate turns negative amid global tensions and poor labor figures. Institutions may trigger a bull run below $75,000.
data.description: Market sentiment is being negatively impacted by geopolitical tension and weak labor data. Currently, institutional buying is happening below $75,000, and it is projected that sellers may soon run out of steam, potentially leading to a bullish market trend.

data.title: Coinbase executives refute lobbying allegations against Bitcoin tax exemption. US lawmakers consider tax breaks for stablecoins, excluding other cryptos.
data.description: Legislators in the United States are considering offering tax exemptions specifically for US dollar stablecoins, which maintain a fixed value and are not subject to fluctuations, excluding other types of cryptocurrencies from this benefit.

data.title: Cryptio, a startup specializing in crypto accounting, secures $45M funding as businesses transition to onchain operations. Surge in tokenized finance fuels demand for blockchain transaction reconciliation systems, emphasizing digital asset accounting.
data.description: The increasing popularity of tokenized finance is fueling a need for platforms that can accurately reconcile blockchain transactions for audits and reporting purposes. This trend has brought digital asset accounting to the forefront, with a growing emphasis on ensuring the accuracy and transparency of financial data in this sector.

data.title: “Save Cuba” — Ordinary Cubans Share Videos of Their Protests and Prayers With Resistance Groups
data.description: In Havana and Miami, amidst record-breaking electricity outages and severe fuel shortages, Cuban citizens are staging some of the most significant protests witnessed on the island since July 11, 2021. Despite facing restricted internet access and limitations on freedom of speech imposed by the authoritarian regime, individuals in Cuba are making their voices heard through bold and unyielding calls for liberty. They are utilizing video messages to communicate their message of defiance and desire for freedom to allies both within and outside the nation. The Assembly of the Cuban R is actively involved in...

data.title: Private Credit Worries Spread To This Major Bank
data.description: North Haven Private Income Fund has recently disclosed that it encountered requests totaling 10.9% of its assets during its quarterly tender offer period, surpassing the 5% limit set by the fund. This development has raised concerns about private credit, with a particular major bank being impacted, as reported by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: How Does Bitcoin Perform During Wars? The Answer May Surprise You
data.description: Bitcoin has demonstrated a consistent trend during global conflicts, historically crashing at the onset of wars but recovering within 50-60 days. Data from 20 geopolitical events shows an average increase of 31.2% in Bitcoin's value as governments inflate money supply to finance conflicts. Bitwise research head Andre Dragosch noted a "Crash-Then-Rally Pattern," with Bitcoin often experiencing price drops at the start of geopolitical risks, only to bounce back and surpass previous levels within 50 days. Studies by Binance Research and BlackRock support these findings, showing an average rebound of 37% within 60 days following major geopolitical incidents. Despite the recent Russia-Ukraine conflict causing Bitcoin to drop from $39,000 to $34,322, it quickly bounced back to $44,000 by March 1st. The pattern of Bitcoin's performance during conflicts has remained consistent as the cryptocurrency has matured.

data.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Falls 700 Points, Utilities Rally; This Oil Sector Giant Hits Profit-Taking Rule (Live Coverage)
data.description: On Thursday, the Dow Jones index experienced a sharp decline of 650 points due to a significant surge in oil prices. Dollar General also faced a decline following disappointing earnings. The stock market today witnessed a 700-point decrease in the Dow, alongside a rally in utilities. A notable oil sector giant was impacted by profit-taking strategies.
